title: Impact of an unexpected oil price increase
section_path: Impact of an unexpected oil price increase
section_path_text: Impact of an unexpected oil price increase
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
The oil shocks of the 1970s, most notably the 1973 oil crisis and the 1979 oil crisis, underscored the pivotal role of energy within the global economic framework, at a time when oil intensity was markedly higher than it is today. Although subsequent decades have brought about structural transformation, technological advancement, and a degree of diversification in the energy mix, the fundamental importance of energy has by no means diminished. It remains an indispensable input across production chains, transportation networks, and global trade flows.
